Though Jacob claimed to have no memory of the incident, he Googled DUI manslaughter on his phone at 2:37 p.m., less than 10 minutes before his father reached out to deputies.

At the time the Ford F250 was seized by police, detectives were unable to prove that Jacob was behind the wheel at the time of the crash and couldn't get a warrant for a sample of Jacob's blood.
DNA taken from the broken headlight of the truck would later come back as a match to 43-year-old Lisa Mancuso.
After a warrant for his arrest was issued, 20-year-old Jacob Moore, in the company of his lawyer, surrendered voluntarily at the police station.
His bond was set at $200,000.
In April of 2025, as part of a plea deal, Jacob pleaded guilty to manslaughter and leaving the scene of an accident resulting in death.
He was later sentenced in 2025 to serve four years in the Arizona Department of Corrections.
He was also ordered to pay restitution to Lisa Mancuso's family and will be placed on three years of supervised probation following his release.
For the one-year anniversary of her death, Lisa Mancuso's friends and family gathered to honor her memory with a 5K, with runners wearing purple, Lisa's favorite color.
